evals.expected_output: A structured accessibility audit report in markdown with severity-classified findings (CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW), WCAG criterion references, concrete JS remediation code, and a full WCAG 2.2 criterion checklist
expectations: Report contains an Executive Summary section with a findings count table by severity
Toggle buttons are flagged as CRITICAL or HIGH for missing keyboard operability (WCAG 2.1.1)
Toggle buttons are flagged for missing aria-pressed attribute
Progress dots are flagged for being purely visual with no accessible state (WCAG 1.3.1)
Step changes via refreshStep() are flagged for not being announced to screen readers (WCAG 4.1.3)
Missing focus indicators (outline:none) are flagged as HIGH (WCAG 2.4.7)
Missing skip navigation is flagged (WCAG 2.4.1)
Missing ARIA landmarks are flagged (WCAG 1.3.1)
Color-only state indication on toggles is flagged (WCAG 1.4.1)
Each finding includes concrete remediation code, not just a description
Report includes a WCAG 2.2 criterion checklist with PASS/FAIL/N-A for each criterion
Report includes a Remediation Roadmap table prioritized by severity
Findings are organized by severity tier (CRITICAL first, then HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW)
Report is saved as a markdown file

evals.expected_output: Accessibility audit report with React-specific findings including SPA focus management, div-onClick anti-pattern, missing focus trap in modal, and recommendations for React Aria or Radix UI
expectations: Navigation div-onClick elements are flagged as CRITICAL for not being keyboard accessible (WCAG 2.1.1)
Missing SPA route change focus management is flagged (unique to React SPAs)
Modal missing focus trap is flagged as CRITICAL or HIGH (WCAG 2.4.3 / 2.1.2)
Modal missing aria-modal, role=dialog, and accessible name is flagged
Report recommends React-specific solutions (React Aria hooks, Radix UI, or useRef/useEffect patterns)
Report includes useFocusOnRouteChange or equivalent focus management pattern
Sidebar nav missing semantic elements (should use nav, a, or button) is flagged
Missing ARIA landmarks in layout is flagged (no main, nav, header)
Each finding includes JSX remediation code
Report includes the WCAG 2.2 criterion checklist

evals.prompt:
can you do an a11y audit of this Angular component? it's a data table with sortable columns and inline editing. We're using Angular 17 with standalone components but NOT using Angular Material. ```typescript @Component({ selector: 'app-data-table', standalone: true, template: `
{{ col.label }} {{ sortColumn === col.key ? (sortDir === 'asc' ? '▲' : '▼') : '' }}
{{ row[col.key] }}
Showing {{ filteredRows.length }} of {{ rows.length }} results
` }) ``` Sort indicators are unicode arrows only. Inline edit triggers on double-click with no keyboard equivalent. Search input has no label. Status bar updates silently.
evals.expected_output: Audit report with Angular-specific findings including missing label associations, double-click-only editing, sort indicators, and recommendations for Angular CDK A11y (LiveAnnouncer, FocusMonitor)
expectations: Search input missing label/aria-label is flagged (WCAG 1.3.1 or 3.3.2)
Sortable column headers using (click) on th without keyboard handler is flagged
Sort direction indicated only by unicode arrows (visual-only) is flagged (WCAG 1.3.1)
Double-click-only inline editing is flagged as CRITICAL or HIGH for no keyboard equivalent (WCAG 2.1.1)
Status bar not announced to screen readers is flagged — should use aria-live or LiveAnnouncer (WCAG 4.1.3)
Report recommends Angular CDK A11y patterns (LiveAnnouncer, FocusMonitor)
Table missing scope attributes on th elements is flagged
Each finding includes Angular template/TypeScript remediation code
Report includes the WCAG 2.2 criterion checklist
Findings use the severity classification system (CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW)
